food_subgroup: Stir-fried rice with sweet soy sauce and egg (Indonesian style)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Nasi Goreng. Nearest parent is a fried rice dish (composite grain-based mixed dish). A new FoodOn concept is recommended for Nasi Goreng as a distinct Indonesian national dish.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite rice dishes — stir-fried rice with sweet soy sauce and egg (closest parent match)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Nasi Goreng. A03SL (composite rice dishes) is the closest defensible parent. Exact code should be requested from EFSA.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for Nasi Goreng as of 2026-04.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker-stall plate of Nasi Goreng weighs approximately 350 g, comprising fried rice, a fried egg, and garnish of cucumber and tomato slices.

nutrient_source.title: Nasi Goreng — HPB Singapore Foodo Food Code F05100083; cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo Food Code F05100083 provides an exact match for Nasi Goreng (Indonesian hawker style) as prepared and sold in Singapore hawker centres. FAO/INFOODS SEA table used for cross-validation of micronutrient values.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — white rice (fried); Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nasi Goreng (Indonesian hawker style) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 is estimated from the closest analogous food: white rice (fried), GI ~70 per University of Sydney GI Database. Kecap manis (sweet soy sauce) contributes additional sugar (~4.8 g/100 g), which may marginally elevate the GI. The presence of protein (egg, meat), fat (cooking oil), and dietary fibre moderates the glycaemic response relative to plain white rice. Glycaemic load per 350 g serving = (70 × 24.2 × 3.5) / 100 = 59.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — 518 mg per 100 g; approximately 1,813 mg per standard 350 g serving, representing ~79% of the 2,300 mg daily recommended limit
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Nasi Goreng to a reduced portion (≤200 g) and request preparation with reduced soy sauce and kecap manis to keep sodium intake below 600 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic index (~70) from jasmine rice base combined with 4.8 g sugar per 100 g from kecap manis; glycaemic load of ~59 per standard 350 g serving is clinically significant
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict Nasi Goreng to a maximum 200 g portion, request reduced kecap manis, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (6.5 g/100 g) with relatively low saturated fat (1.2 g/100 g); cholesterol 72 mg/100 g; high sodium raises blood pressure risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be cautious of the high sodium content (~1,813 mg per serving) and choose a smaller portion (≤250 g) while requesting less soy sauce to reduce cardiovascular risk from sodium-driven hypertension.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nasi Goreng (Indonesian Hawker Style) is Indonesia's iconic national fried rice dish, widely served at hawker centres, mamak stalls, and Indonesian restaurants across Southeast Asia. The dish is made from day-old jasmine rice stir-fried in a hot wok with shallots, garlic, and bird's eye chilli, then seasoned with kecap manis (sweet Indonesian soy sauce) that imparts a characteristic deep brown colour and subtly sweet, smoky flavour. It is typically topped with a sunny-side-up fried egg and garnished with fresh cucumber slices and tomato wedges. Some hawker variants include prawn crackers (kerupuk) on the side. Per 100 g, Nasi Goreng provides approximately 173 kcal, 5.5 g protein, 6.5 g fat, and 24.2 g carbohydrate, with 518 mg sodium — making sodium the primary clinical concern. The glycaemic index is approximately 70 (high), driven by the jasmine rice base and kecap manis sugar content, yielding a glycaemic load of ~59 per standard 350 g serving. This dish is halal and dairy-free but contains egg and soy allergens. It is not suitable for diabetics in standard portions and requires sodium reduction for hypertensive individuals. The kecap manis distinguishes Nasi Goreng from Chinese fried rice, making it sweeter and more aromatic.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo Food Code F05100083 (exact match) and cross-validated against FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (white rice fried, GI 70) with documented estimation basis. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Nasi Goreng (Indonesian hawker style) is not separately reported in HPB Singapore Foodo F05100083 or FAO/INFOODS SEA table as of 2026-04. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary source for this composite dish. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source). GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white rice (fried) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Nasi Goreng hawker style exists in the database. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 24.2 × 3.5) / 100 = 59.2. Copper (0.08 mg/100 g), manganese (0.12 mg/100 g), and vitamin K (1.2 mcg/100 g) sourced from enriched_json with confidence 0.9; these values should be verified against USDA FoodData Central in a future revision. The description in the input JSON conflates Nasi Goreng USA (a Malaysian mamak variant) with the Indonesian hawker style; the canonical record has been aligned to the Indonesian hawker style as indicated by the food name and HPB food code. Health suitability for hypertension is flagged as high concern despite the input record marking it suitable=true, because 1,813 mg sodium per 350 g serving represents 79% of the daily recommended limit, which is clinically significant for hypertensive individuals.
